title: The cyclo driver of Hue
summary: An Australian visitor in Hue asks her cyclo driver for the 'best' street, and gets a different answer than expected.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: Good morning, madam. Where would you like to go today? The citadel? The river? The tombs?

body.speaker: Helen
body.text: Good morning, Mr Tuan. I have already seen the citadel. I have seen the river. I want to see the best street in Hue. The most beautiful one.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: [Mr Tuan thinks for a moment.] The most beautiful street, or the best street? They are not the same.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: The most beautiful street is in the old part, near the river. Many tourists go there. There are flowers and old shops. The best street is not famous. It is a small street where I drink coffee every morning. It is six minutes from here.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: Because three things happen there at half past seven. An old lady sells sticky rice from a basket. A man feeds two cats with one piece of fish. A small girl walks to school past my coffee shop. She says good morning to everyone.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: Yes. Three small things, every day, twenty-six years. I came to Hue from a village in 1999. I sat on this street my first morning. The street is my best.

body.speaker: Helen
body.text: Can we go there instead of the beautiful one?

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: We can. But you must promise: do not take a photograph. The girl will not be a girl if there is a camera.

body.speaker: Mr Tuan
body.text: Then sit back. We arrive in six minutes. The sticky rice is still warm at half past seven.

annotations.meaningVi: xích lô (xe ba bánh chở khách)
con phố đẹp nhất — so sánh nhất với tính từ dài

grammarNotes.title: Superlatives with 'the most + long adjective'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng 'the most + tính từ dài' cho so sánh nhất. Trong bài: 'the best street in Hue. The most beautiful one.' 'Best' là dạng so sánh nhất của 'good' (bất quy tắc); 'most beautiful' dùng cho tính từ dài ('beautiful' có 3 âm tiết). Quy tắc chung: tính từ ngắn (1 âm tiết) — '-est' (small → smallest); tính từ dài (2+ âm tiết) — 'most' (most beautiful). Một số bất quy tắc: good → best, bad → worst, far → furthest.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 forms superlatives of long adjectives with 'the most + adjective.' From the dialogue: 'the best street in Hue. The most beautiful one.' 'Best' is the irregular superlative of 'good'; 'most beautiful' is used for long adjectives ('beautiful' has 3 syllables). Rule of thumb: short adjectives (1 syllable) take '-est' (small → smallest); long adjectives (2+ syllables) take 'most' (most beautiful). Irregulars: good → best, bad → worst, far → furthest.

translationVi:
Ông Tuấn: Chào bà. Hôm nay bà muốn đi đâu? Kinh thành? Sông? Các lăng?

Helen: Chào ông Tuấn. Tôi đã xem kinh thành rồi. Tôi đã xem sông rồi. Tôi muốn xem con phố đẹp nhất ở Huế.

Ông Tuấn: [Ông Tuấn nghĩ một lát.] Con phố đẹp nhất hay con phố hay nhất? Không giống nhau đâu.

Helen: Ồ. Khác nhau thế nào?

Ông Tuấn: Con phố đẹp nhất ở khu cổ, gần sông. Nhiều khách du lịch đến đó. Có hoa, có những cửa hàng cổ, có ảnh trong mỗi quán cà phê. Con phố hay nhất thì không nổi tiếng. Đó là một phố nhỏ nơi tôi uống cà phê mỗi sáng. Cách đây sáu phút.

Helen: Sao nó hay nhất?

Ông Tuấn: Vì ba điều xảy ra ở đó lúc bảy giờ rưỡi. Một bà cụ bán xôi từ một cái rổ. Một người đàn ông cho hai con mèo ăn bằng một miếng cá. Một bé gái đi học ngang quán cà phê tôi. Bé luôn chào tôi. Bé không biết tôi. Bé chào tất cả mọi người.

Helen: Ba điều nhỏ.

Ông Tuấn: Vâng. Ba điều nhỏ, mỗi ngày, hai mươi sáu năm. Tôi đến Huế từ một ngôi làng năm 1999. Tôi ngồi ở phố đó sáng đầu tiên. Tôi ngồi ở đó bây giờ. Con phố đó là 'hay nhất' của tôi.

Helen: Mình đến đó thay vì phố đẹp được không?

Ông Tuấn: Được. Nhưng bà phải hứa: đừng chụp ảnh. Bé sẽ không còn là bé nếu có máy ảnh.

Helen: Tôi hứa.

Ông Tuấn: Vậy ngồi lại. Sáu phút nữa tới. Xôi vẫn còn ấm lúc bảy rưỡi.

questions.explanation: Title and context — he drives the cyclo.
'I have seen the citadel. I have seen the river.'

questions.prompt: What does Helen ask to see?
options: The best street, the most beautiful one in Hue.
questions.explanation: 'I want to see the best street in Hue.'

questions.prompt: What distinction does Mr Tuan make?
options: Between the most beautiful street (for tourists) and the best street (his daily one).

questions.prompt: Where is the most beautiful street?
options: In the old part, near the river.
questions.explanation: 'The most beautiful street is in the old part, near the river.'

questions.prompt: Why is the best street the best, for Mr Tuan?
options: Because three small things happen there at half past seven (sticky rice woman, cat-feeder, girl who says good morning).
Because it has the cheapest coffee.
Because the road is the smoothest.
Because it has the most shops.

questions.prompt: How long has Mr Tuan been doing this?
questions.explanation: 'Twenty-six years. I came to Hue from a village in 1999.'

options: To go to the best street instead of the beautiful one.
To go to the beautiful one first.
questions.explanation: 'Can we go there instead of the beautiful one?'

questions.prompt: What does Mr Tuan ask Helen to promise?
Why does Mr Tuan not want a photo?
options: 'The girl will not be a girl if there is a camera.'
Photos are forbidden by the city.
A tourist asks for 'the most beautiful' street and is offered a deeper version of beautiful: the one her driver has chosen to love every morning.
Hue has the prettiest streets in Vietnam.

cloze.template:
Mr Tuan: Good morning, madam. Where would you like to go today? The {{b1}}? The river? The tombs?
Helen: Good morning, Mr Tuan. I have already seen the citadel. I have seen the river. I want to see the {{b2}} street in Hue. The most beautiful one.
Mr Tuan: The most beautiful street, or the best street? They are not the same.
Helen: Oh. What is the difference?
Mr Tuan: The most beautiful street is in the {{b3}} part, near the river. Many tourists go there. There are flowers and old shops. The best street is not famous. It is a small street where I drink coffee every morning. It is six minutes from here.
Helen: Why is it the best?
Mr Tuan: Because three things happen there at half past {{b4}}. An old lady sells sticky {{b5}} from a basket. A man feeds two cats with one piece of {{b6}}. A small girl walks to school past my coffee shop. She says good morning to everyone.
Helen: Three small things.
Mr Tuan: Yes. Three small things, every day, twenty-six {{b7}}. I came to Hue from a village in 1999. I sat on this street my first morning. The street is my best.
Helen: Can we go there {{b8}} of the beautiful one?
Mr Tuan: We can. But you must promise: do not take a {{b9}}. The girl will not be a girl if there is a camera.
Helen: I promise.
Mr Tuan: Then sit back. We arrive in six minutes. The sticky rice is still {{b10}} at half past seven.

blanks.explanation: The old part, near the river.
Still warm at half past seven.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Tuan separates 'most beautiful' from 'best'. The first belongs to tourists; the second is what he chose to love daily. Is there a place in your life that is your 'best' without being the most beautiful — and could you take a friend there without spoiling it?
